svg.title: Narrative Visualization of Naive Bayes Classifier

rect.style: fill: #fff; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);

g.text: Naive Bayes Classifiers for Text Classification and Sentiment Analysis

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle; opacity: 0; transform-origin: top center;

g.data-narration: Naive Bayes is a family of probabilistic classifiers that are widely used for text classification and sentiment analysis.

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle;

g.data-narration: It is a type of Bayesian classifier that assumes independence between features.

rect.style: fill: #fff; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle; opacity: 0; transform-origin: top center;

g.data-narration: Naive Bayes is a family of probabilistic classifiers that are widely used for text classification and sentiment analysis.

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle;

g.data-narration: It is a type of Bayesian classifier that assumes independence between features.

rect.style: fill: #fff; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle; opacity: 0; transform-origin: top center;

g.data-narration: The Naive Bayes algorithm starts by choosing a prior distribution over the class labels.

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle;

g.data-narration: This distribution is typically a uniform distribution.

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle; opacity: 0; transform-origin: top center;

g.data-narration: The algorithm then calculates the likelihood of each feature given the class label.

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle;

g.data-narration: For example, if we are classifying text as positive or negative, the likelihood of each word in the text given the class label is calculated.

rect.style: fill: #fff; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle; opacity: 0; transform-origin: top center;

g.data-narration: There are several types of Naive Bayes classifiers, including Multinomial Naive Bayes and Bernoulli Naive Bayes.

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle;

g.data-narration: Multinomial Naive Bayes assumes that each feature is a multinomial distribution.

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle; opacity: 0; transform-origin: top center;

g.data-narration: Bernoulli Naive Bayes assumes that each feature is a Bernoulli distribution.

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle;

g.data-narration: For example, if we are classifying text as positive or negative, the likelihood of each word in the text given the class label is calculated.

rect.style: fill: #fff; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle; opacity: 0; transform-origin: top center;

g.data-narration: In summary, Naive Bayes is a family of probabilistic classifiers that are widely used for text classification and sentiment analysis.

data-narration.style: fill: #666; font-size: 18; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle;

g.data-narration: It is a type of Bayesian classifier that assumes independence between features.
